title: Galaxy SED Fitting
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Galaxy SED Fitting: Galaxy SED fitting: infer stellar mass, SFR, age, metallicity from broadband photometry using stellar population synthesis models. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.sed.stellar_population…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~80; AGN_contamination, stellar_IMF_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: F_lambda = integral SFH(t) * SSP_lambda(t, Z) dt * 10^(-0.4*A_lambda)
E.world_state_x: galaxy parameters: M_star, SFR, age, metallicity Z, dust A_V
E.observation_y: broadband fluxes F_nu in N_filters bands from UV to IR

W.regime: Existence of the recovered galaxy_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 80); AGN_contamination dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Observation gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: sigma(log M_star) < 0.2 dex, sigma(log SFR) < 0.3 dex at SNR > 5. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~80. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |AGN_contamination|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_models * N_filters) for chi2 grid per iteration
